ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION, 503, DEFENDANT GIOVANETTI, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, SUBMITTED ON JAN-08-2015 BY TERI L. JACKSON. HEARING HELD. THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT GIOVANETTI, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT UNDER SUBMISSION. PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED A PROPOSED ORDER. DEFENDANT WILL SUBMIT ITS PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: CLAUDIA J. KNAP OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-08-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT GIOVANETTI INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS HEARD AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 1/8/15. THE COURT ISSUED A RULING DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION. REFER TO SIGNED ORDER FOR DETAILS OF RULING. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON (503/TLJ)

calendar.matters:
Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Roberts Brothers Construction Co.
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Compmany.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Nichole M. Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Daniel Westbrook, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Paul Westbrook, Deceased, and Suezette Lowery, Linda Hursh, Pamela Eckhardt, as Legal Heirs of Paul Westbrook, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Roberts Brothers Construction Co.
Default was entered against defendant on October 2, 2014.
Statement of Damages was filed and served on October 2, 2014.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Daniel Westbrook, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Paul Westbrook, Deceased, and Suezette Lowery, Linda Hursh, Pamela Eckhardt, as Legal Heirs of Paul Westbrook, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Roberts Brothers Construction Co., as follows:

Economic Damages: $2,671.24.
Non-Economic Damages - $4,000,000.00.
Total - $4,002671.24.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
